danger_signals.visual_form: Armed humanoid figures in patrol patterns, distinctive armor/uniforms
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring evasion or non-lethal neutralization

danger_signals.visual_form: Bright illuminated zones contrasting with shadows
danger_signals.meaning: High visibility = danger of detection; player character exposed
Technological threats that patrol autonomously; Mechanist faction control
Detection risk; presence of Mechanist technological surveillance

danger_signals.visual_form: Hard surfaces (metal, stone) versus soft surfaces (moss, carpet)
danger_signals.meaning: Audible threat; footsteps alert guards to player presence

safety_signals.visual_form: Dark areas, unlit zones, areas with minimal light sources
safety_signals.meaning: Safety from detection; player visibility minimized
Player character not visible to enemies; stealth maintained

safety_signals.visual_form: Soft surfaces, moss-covered ground, carpeted areas
Corridors and areas devoid of moving guards
High ground with dark cover, rooflines, alcoves
safety_signals.meaning: Vantage points for observation and evasion

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Guards equipped with advanced armor and technology-based weapons; patrol in organized formations; operate automated security systems
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Heavy use of metal construction, steam technology, automated lights, surveillance cameras, industrial architecture; presence of automatons and mechanical devices; organized, geometric cityscape elements

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Armed religious zealots; present in earlier game but Mechanist schism has fragmented this faction
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Religious architecture, hammers as visual motif, temples and sanctuaries

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Driven into wilderness; engage in guerrilla warfare against Mechanists; displaced from urban environments
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Presence in wilderness beyond City; organic, natural design elements; contrasts with technological Mechanist zones

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Dormant at game start; manipulative and secretive faction
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Hidden locations, mysterious architecture; visual presence minimal early in game

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Heavy technological presence; automated security systems visible; steam-powered devices throughout City; organized guard patrols with advanced equipment; industrial aesthetic dominates
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased guard vigilance; automated patrols; systematic security sweeps; heightened police presence; mechanized enforcement

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Wilderness areas contrast sharply with urban tech-zones; natural elements at periphery of City; evidence of conflict at borders
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Pagan fighters engage in hit-and-run tactics; displaced from organized urban society; resistance activities visible in mission environments

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Religious architecture shows signs of conflict/division; some temples converted to Mechanist purposes; visual distinction between traditional and mechanized religious spaces
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Fractured faction identity; some guards remain traditional, others adopt Mechanist technology; conflicting patrol patterns and doctrines

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mysterious faction symbols present but not actively integrated into environmental control; hidden sanctuaries and secret locations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Minimal overt presence; information gathering rather than active enforcement; manipulation occurs behind scenes

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Heavy guard presence throughout City; organized checkpoints; technological surveillance integrated into architecture; official insignia and uniforms visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Systematic patrols; coordinated security protocols; use of technology for tracking and detection; authoritarian control structures

environmental_storytelling.example: Mechanist zones feature geometric, industrial design with visible gears and steam technology; contrast with older medieval-style structures reveals power shift and technological takeover
Heavily lit Mechanist-controlled areas with bright torches and mechanical lights versus shadow-filled older districts; visual manifestation of surveillance society
Mechanist guards wear advanced technological armor; appearance of automatons indicates faction control intensity; equipment tells story of resource allocation and power
Wilderness at City edges showing conflict between natural world and technology; evidence of guerrilla warfare and Pagan resistance in border regions
Temples repurposed with Mechanist technology; mixed religious iconography shows factional schism and ideological conflict
Metal flooring = loud, dangerous; moss/soft surfaces = safe; material choices in level design communicate safety versus danger zones

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Follows systematic patrol routes; uses technological aids (lights, communication); organized movement patterns; responds to alerts with coordinated response
Investigates noises; increases light sources; communicates with other guards; systematic search patterns
Continuous autonomous patrol; mechanical movement; predictable patterns; detects based on proximity and light exposure
Fixed or rotating observation; constant monitoring; immediate alert triggers on detection
Guerrilla tactics; irregular patrol patterns; engagement with Mechanist forces; presence outside organized City structure
Light monitor shows zero visibility; safe from detection; can observe and plan
Light monitor shows increased visibility; high detection risk; requires evasion or action
Audibility increases; footsteps alert guards; requires stealth or evasion

notes: Thief II communicates world-state primarily through visual hierarchy of light versus shadow, architectural aesthetics tied to faction control, and the presence of technology-enhanced security. The game's stealth paradigm makes visibility/invisibility the primary danger signal. Faction dominance is shown through environmental design choices: Mechanist control manifests in industrial architecture, automated security, and bright artificial lighting. The post-Trickster world-state is communicated through the visual prevalence of technology and organized authority structures. The player's HUD light monitor serves as a real-time world-state indicator, directly translating environmental visibility into mechanical feedback. Guard behavior and equipment design telegraph faction allegiance and threat level.
